target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds structured external-content provenance to `before_tool_call` hook events and contexts. Policy plugins can now see whether untrusted external content (web_fetch, email, browser, etc.) was present in the model context that led to a tool call.

## What changed

- **New detector** in `src/agents/tool-hook-external-content.ts`: parses production `<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="...">>>` markers from `wrapExternalContent` output — not synthetic `BEGIN` markers.
- **New types** in `src/plugins/hook-types.ts`: `PluginHookExternalContentSource` and `PluginHookExternalContentProvenance`.
- **Hook propagation** in `src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.ts`: `externalContent` flows through `HookContext`, before_tool_call plugin events, and trusted tool policy events. Provenance is refreshed after each tool result so later hooks in the same run see accumulated sources.
- **Shared hook context** in `src/agents/agent-tools.ts`: `createOpenClawCodingTools` accepts an optional `beforeToolCallHookContext` so callers can share provenance state across wrapped tool instances. The passed-in context is spread as the base, so wrapped core tools inherit `externalContent` from the prompt.
- **Prompt provenance** in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`: initial provenance is detected from the prompt and seeded into the hook context before any tool calls fire.
- **Docs** in `docs/plugins/hooks.md`: documented the new `externalContent` field and source semantics.
- **Bug fix**: `structuredClone` in `recordAdjustedParamsForToolCall` now wrapped in try/catch so hostile proxy objects don't break tool execution.
- **Anti-spoofing fix**: `Source:` labels are only read from the metadata section (between start marker and `---` separator), not from the untrusted content body.

## Why

External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text by `wrapExternalContent`. Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary had no structured signal showing whether external content was present. Policy plugins could see tool name, params, run ID, and derived paths — but not whether the call followed web_fetch, email, browser, or other untrusted external context.

## Production detector

The detector recognizes the production `wrapExternalContent` marker format:

```
<<<EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="abc123">>>
Source: Web Fetch
---
sanitized content
<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="abc123">>>
```

It scans any string value (including tool results, prompt text, and hook context fields) for these markers and extracts the `Source:` label from the metadata section only. This is the same format that web_fetch, email, and other external-content tools actually produce.

## Wiring fix (ClawSweeper P1)

Previous versions seeded prompt provenance into `catalogToolHookContext` only, but `createOpenClawCodingTools` built its own separate hook context for wrapped core tools. The first real tool call (exec, read, etc.) after an external-content prompt could reach policies without `externalContent`.

Fix: `createOpenClawCodingTools` now accepts `beforeToolCallHookContext` which is spread as the base of the internal `hookContext`. `attempt.ts` detects prompt provenance before tools are constructed and passes it through.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: External content provenance must survive a real same-run path where a `web_fetch` tool result adds wrapped untrusted content, and a later tool call reaches a trusted policy hook with structured `externalContent` provenance.

Real environment tested: PR head `940416f19a3c5d74c8a87275dd6f473a8215495a`, local Linux worktree `/tmp/openclaw-pr-91800`, Node.js v24.15.0.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
npx tsx /tmp/openclaw-runtime-proof.mts
```

The temporary proof harness imported production modules and used the real runtime path:
- `createWebFetchTool`
- `wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook`
- `refreshHookContextExternalContentFromToolResult`
- `createExecTool`
- active registry `trustedToolPolicy.evaluate`

The harness installed a trusted tool policy in the active plugin registry, wrapped a real `web_fetch` tool and a real `exec` tool with the same mutable hook context, fetched `https://example.com/`, then ran `printf 'runtime proof exec reached\n'`. Output below is redacted to omit local filesystem details and keeps only tool names, run ids, call ids, and provenance.

Evidence after fix: redacted terminal output from the real runtime proof command.
```json
{
  "proof": "external-content provenance reaches later trusted policy hook",
  "prHead": "940416f19a3c5d74c8a87275dd6f473a8215495a",
  "runtimePath": [
    "createWebFetchTool",
    "wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook",
    "refreshHookContextExternalContentFromToolResult",
    "createExecTool",
    "trustedToolPolicy.evaluate"
  ],
  "fetchedFrom": "https://example.com/",
  "fetchResultHadProductionMarker": true,
  "sharedHookContextExternalContent": {
    "present": true,
    "sources": ["web_fetch"]
  },
  "capturedPolicyEvents": [
    {
      "phase": "web_fetch",
      "event": {
        "toolName": "web_fetch",
        "runId": "runtime-proof-web-fetch-then-exec",
        "toolCallId": "call-web-fetch-1"
      },
      "ctx": {
        "toolName": "web_fetch",
        "runId": "runtime-proof-web-fetch-then-exec",
        "toolCallId": "call-web-fetch-1"
      }
    },
    {
      "phase": "exec",
      "event": {
        "toolName": "exec",
        "runId": "runtime-proof-web-fetch-then-exec",
        "toolCallId": "call-exec-2",
        "externalContent": {
          "present": true,
          "sources": ["web_fetch"]
        }
      },
      "ctx": {
        "toolName": "exec",
        "runId": "runtime-proof-web-fetch-then-exec",
        "toolCallId": "call-exec-2",
        "externalContent": {
          "present": true,
          "sources": ["web_fetch"]
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Supplemental targeted regression verification:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts \
  src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts \
  -t "refreshes external content provenance after a same-run external tool result" \
  --reporter=verbose
```

Result:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  1 passed | 56 skipped (57)
  Duration  2.08s (transform 802ms, setup 159ms, import 1.57s, tests 76ms, environment 0ms)
```

Observed result after fix:
- The first `web_fetch` trusted policy hook correctly has no `externalContent` yet.
- The real `web_fetch` result contains production external-content markers.
- The shared hook context is refreshed to `{"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}` after `web_fetch` returns.
- The later real `exec` tool call reaches `trustedToolPolicy.evaluate` with `event.externalContent` and `ctx.externalContent` set to `{"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}`.
- Regression coverage also exercises front-matter `---`, multiple production wrapper blocks, JSON-string rendered tool payloads, and forged `Source: Email` text inside untrusted content bodies.

Not tested: Telegram desktop integration, iOS/macOS builds, Windows service install paths.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
